evidence.velocity_vs_rigour: Introduce robust CI/CD frameworks, unit testing, and security protocols
evidence.velocity_vs_rigour_explanation: Testing, compliance, and security frameworks dominate the responsibilities with no velocity-oriented language present.
evidence.domain_risk: Ensure PCI-DSS/SOC2 compliance while optimizing costs
evidence.domain_risk_explanation: Explicit PCI-DSS/SOC2 compliance language in a payments company places this at high domain risk.
evidence.data_team_maturity: Build and scale data warehouse core and pipelines utilizing BigQuery and managed cloud services
evidence.data_team_maturity_explanation: The two-phase framing (build infrastructure, then own semantic layer) and 'from scratch' language signal a still-forming data function.
evidence.jd_authorship: Own the semantic layer, writing production-grade LookML
evidence.jd_authorship_explanation: Precise tools (BigQuery, LookML, Cloud Composer/Airflow, Dataform) and phased scope give hiring-manager-level detail.
evidence.stakeholder_orientation: Build complex data models serving clean data to AI models, routing engines, and BI tools
evidence.stakeholder_orientation_explanation: The primary audience is internal data consumers — AI models, routing engines, and the BI layer itself.
evidence.autonomy_level: Own the semantic layer, writing production-grade LookML; Maintain Looker and business intelligence layer with full ownership
evidence.autonomy_level_explanation: Full ownership language over both infrastructure build and the semantic layer signals strategic scope.
evidence.ai_role: Build complex data models serving clean data to AI models, routing engines, and BI tools; Prepare data ecosystem for real-time AI modeling
evidence.ai_role_explanation: The candidate builds the data infrastructure that downstream AI models and routing engines consume, making this AI-enabler work.
evidence.testing_framing: Introduce robust CI/CD frameworks, unit testing, and security protocols
evidence.testing_framing_explanation: Unit testing and CI/CD frameworks are framed as something the candidate introduces and owns, not just a listed tool.
evidence.loss_aversion_framing: Ensure PCI-DSS/SOC2 compliance while optimizing costs; Handle confidential data per organizational security policies
evidence.loss_aversion_framing_explanation: High: PCI-DSS/SOC2 compliance and confidential data handling appear as core, repeated responsibilities in a payments context.
evidence.greenfield_vs_fix: Proven track record building data warehouses and semantic layers from scratch
evidence.greenfield_vs_fix_explanation: Explicit 'from scratch' language and a two-phase build-then-own structure make this a greenfield mandate.
evidence.loss_aversion: PCI-DSS/SOC2 compliance and confidential data handling are named as core ongoing responsibilities in a payments company, indicating high regulatory exposure.
